name: Admin User Remote Logon Detected
description: Detects a successful remote logon (logon type 10) using the Negotiate authentication package by administrative users whose names follow the 'Admin-*' pattern. This could indicate a legitimate administrative activity or an attacker using a compromised administrative account.

prompts.prompt: A successful remote logon (type 10) was detected on the host **{workstation_name}**. The logon was performed by an administrative user, **{target_user_name}**, from the IP address **{ip_address}**. The authentication package used was **{authentication_package_name}**. This activity is a potential indicator of **Initial Access** or **Lateral Movement** using a valid administrative account (**MITRE ATT&CK T1078**). While this may be a legitimate administrative action, it is also a common technique for adversaries. Immediate verification of this logon with the user or IT administrator is required to confirm its legitimacy. This event should be treated as a high-priority alert until a benign explanation is confirmed.

parameters.name: target_user_name
parameters.description: The administrative user account that logged on remotely.

parameters.name: ip_address
parameters.description: The source IP address from which the logon originated.

parameters.name: authentication_package_name
parameters.description: The authentication package used for the logon.

parameters.name: workstation_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
